content: Synthetic folic acid is different from natural food folate and requires metabolism that natural folates don't require.
Folate deficiency and vitamin B12 deficiency can both cause macrocytic anemia, characterized by larger than normal red blood cells.
Methylated folate supports red blood cell development, while natural food folate is predominantly in the unmethylated form.
Synthetic folic acid is less likely to cause folate deficiency anemia if B12 status is adequate, as B12 can methylate folate.
Synthetic folic acid is more stable and easier to digest compared to natural food folates, leading to higher bioavailability.
Eating refined grains or taking synthetic folic acid supplements can mask folate deficiency symptoms if B12 status is low.
